canonical_definition: The Indirect Object Identification task, used to evaluate model understanding of internal circuits by testing if a model can identify the indirect object in sentences with a known structure.
papers: Rethinking Evaluation of Sparse Autoencoders through the Representation of Polysemous Words
Towards Principled Evaluations of Sparse Autoencoders for Interpretability and Control

definitions.text: The Indirect Object Identification task, used to evaluate model understanding of internal circuits by testing if a model can identify the indirect object in sentences with a known structure.
definitions.paper: Rethinking Evaluation of Sparse Autoencoders through the Representation of Polysemous Words

relationships.paper: Towards Principled Evaluations of Sparse Autoencoders for Interpretability and Control
relationships.evidence: In Wang et al. (2022), the authors analyze how the decoder-only transformer language model GPT-2 Small (Radford et al., 2019) performs the Indirect Object Identification (IOI) task. (Section 3)

iclr25: Towards Principled Evaluations of Sparse Autoencoders for Interpretability and Control
